class.name: BluetoothManager
class.brief_description: Cross-platform Bluetooth device discovery, pairing, and connection management.
class.description: [BluetoothManager] is the main GDExtension node for BluetoothGD. Add it to your scene tree (or register it as an autoload) to scan for devices, pair gamepads and other peripherals, query connection state, and react to platform events through signals. All Bluetooth I/O runs on a background worker thread; results are delivered on the main thread via signals during [method _process]. [b]Supported platforms:[/b] Windows (WinRT), Linux (BlueZ). macOS is stubbed. [b]Device dictionary fields[/b] used by [signal device_found], [method get_discovered_devices], and [method get_paired_devices]: [codeblock lang=text] address — MAC string "AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF" when known, otherwise a platform device id name — Human-readable name from the OS paired — Whether the device is paired with the system connected — Whether the device is currently connected trusted — Whether the device is trusted (Linux) device_class — Inferred class, e.g. "gamepad" or "unknown" device_id — Platform-specific identifier (Windows AEP id, Linux object path) rssi — Signal strength in dBm when available (Linux scan) [/codeblock] [b]Scan options[/b] passed to [method start_scan]: [codeblock lang=text] named_only — bool, hide devices without a friendly name (default false) gamepads_only — bool, only report gamepad-class devices (default false) min_rssi — int, minimum RSSI threshold (default -127, Linux) timeout_seconds — int, scan timeout hint (default 0) [/codeblock]

method.name: start_scan
method.param: Optional scan filters. See class description for supported keys.
method.description: Begins discovering Bluetooth devices. Emits [signal scan_started], then [signal device_found] for each device. Already-known paired devices are included. Call [method stop_scan] to end discovery.

method.name: stop_scan
method.description: Stops an active device scan. Emits [signal scan_stopped].

method.name: get_discovered_devices
method.description: Returns an [Array] of device [Dictionary]s discovered during the current session (see class description for fields).

method.name: pair_device
method.param: Device MAC address or platform identifier from [signal device_found].
method.description: Starts pairing with the device at [param address]. Emits [signal pairing_started], then [signal pairing_succeeded] or [signal pairing_failed]. Interactive PIN flows emit [signal pairing_pin_requested] or [signal pairing_confirmation_requested]; respond with [method confirm_pairing], [method reject_pairing], or [method cancel_pairing].

method.name: pair_device_by_id
method.description: Pairs using the platform device id instead of a MAC address. Useful for Windows Association Endpoint ids before an address is resolved.

method.name: unpair_device
method.description: Removes the system pairing for [param address]. On Windows, disconnect HID controllers before unpairing. Emits [signal device_removed] on success.

method.name: refresh_paired_devices
method.description: Re-queries the OS for paired devices and emits [signal devices_refreshed].

method.name: get_paired_devices
method.description: Returns an [Array] of [Dictionary]s for devices currently paired with the system.

method.name: is_paired
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if [param address] is paired.

method.name: connect_device
method.description: Requests a connection to a paired device. HID gamepads often connect automatically once paired and powered on. Emits [signal connection_changed] when state updates.

method.name: disconnect_device
method.description: Attempts to disconnect [param address]. Windows cannot force-disconnect Bluetooth HID gamepads; power off the controller or unpair in system settings. Emits [signal connection_changed] with an explanatory [code]message[/code].

method.name: is_connected
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if [param address] is connected. On Windows, HID gamepads are detected via the HID stack as well as classic Bluetooth.

method.name: confirm_pairing
method.param: PIN to submit when the stack requests one.
method.description: Accepts an interactive pairing request. Pass [param pin] when [signal pairing_pin_requested] was emitted.

method.name: reject_pairing
method.description: Rejects the current interactive pairing request.

method.name: cancel_pairing
method.description: Cancels the in-progress pairing ceremony and rejects any pending PIN/confirmation prompt.

method.name: normalize_address
method.description: Normalizes [param address] to uppercase colon-separated form ([code]AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF[/code]).

method.name: is_valid_bluetooth_address
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if [param address] is a valid 6-byte Bluetooth MAC.

method.name: can_unpair_while_connected
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the current platform allows unpairing while a device is still connected. [code]true[/code] on Windows and Linux; [code]false[/code] on macOS and other stub backends.

method.name: is_bluetooth_available
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the native backend initialized successfully.

method.name: is_radio_on
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the Bluetooth radio is on. Updated when the backend reports [signal error_occurred] with a radio-off error.

method.name: get_platform_name
method.description: Returns the active backend name: [code]"windows"[/code], [code]"linux"[/code], [code]"macos"[/code], or [code]"unknown"[/code].

method.name: get_capabilities
method.description: Returns platform capability flags, including [code]platform[/code], [code]implemented[/code], [code]supports_ble[/code], [code]supports_device_id[/code], [code]supports_rssi[/code] (Linux), [code]can_disconnect_hid[/code], [code]can_unpair_while_connected[/code], and [code]needs_pin_ui[/code].

method.name: get_error_code_name
method.param: Numeric error code from [signal pairing_failed] or [signal error_occurred].
method.description: Converts an error code to a stable string: [code]none[/code], [code]device_not_found[/code], [code]radio_off[/code], [code]not_supported[/code], [code]pin_required[/code], [code]pairing_rejected[/code], [code]permission_denied[/code], [code]operation_timeout[/code], [code]invalid_address[/code], [code]already_in_progress[/code], or [code]unknown[/code].

signal.name: bluetooth_ready
signal.description: Emitted once the backend is initialized and the first paired-device snapshot is available.

signal.name: device_found
signal.description: Emitted when a device is discovered or updated during scan or refresh.

signal.name: device_removed
signal.param: Address or identifier of the removed device.
signal.description: Emitted when a device is unpaired or removed from the system cache.

signal.name: devices_refreshed
signal.description: Emitted after [method refresh_paired_devices] updates the paired-device list.

signal.name: pairing_started
signal.description: Emitted when a pair operation begins.

signal.name: pairing_confirmation_requested
signal.description: Emitted when the OS asks the user to confirm a pairing code or match. Respond with [method confirm_pairing] or [method reject_pairing].

signal.name: pairing_pin_requested
signal.description: Emitted when the OS requests a PIN. Call [method confirm_pairing] with the PIN.

signal.name: pairing_display_pin
signal.param: PIN shown by the remote device.
signal.description: Emitted when the OS displays a PIN for the user to enter on the remote device.

signal.name: connection_changed
signal.param: Optional platform note (e.g. HID disconnect limitations on Windows).
signal.description: Emitted when a device's connection state changes.

signal.name: error_occurred
signal.description: Emitted when a backend operation fails.
